items.note: The full text is public — as a read-out, not a signed PDF — and it buys time: ceasefire, Hormuz and oil relief now; nuclear, Lebanon, enforcement and the toll question deferred to a 60-day clock. Bürgenstock tomorrow is implementation talks.

Blockade off within 30 days, transit toll-free 'for 60 days only'. Three Saudi supertankers (~6M barrels) crossed after signing, but a full restart is weeks off — and Brent's slide is partly trapped barrels clearing into a soft market.

The fault line: the text protects Lebanese sovereignty while Israel — not a party — negotiates to keep troops south of the Litani. Strikes continued on 17-18 June; ~1.2M remain displaced under the process coverage.
The under-covered front: Russian strikes could cut Odesa-port grain volumes from ~6M to ~4M tons a month, and Ukraine's export terminals now compete with winter-grid repair for the same scarce money.

items.what: Switzerland says Friday's event above Lake Lucerne is now implementation talks, not a signing — Iran says the presidents already signed, so the ceremony the calendar promised has quietly become a working session.

IAEA chief Rafael Grossi greeted the pact with a caution that doubles as the whole story: 'Now the technical work starts.' Enrichment and the highly-enriched-uranium stockpile are deferred to the 60-day talks.

JPMorgan pulled Anthropic's Claude from its approved-tool list for Hong Kong staff, following Goldman — the US export order propagating into private corporate access, bank by bank, jurisdiction by jurisdiction.

Markets are pricing a possible US rate hike by October under Chair Kevin Warsh even as oil falls — the peace dividend in energy meeting a hawkish turn in money, pulling the macro story two ways at once.
Zelenskiy cast drone strikes on Russia as retaliation — 'your Moscow will burn' — but the costlier blow is to Odesa's ports, where a third of monthly grain capacity is now in question.
